column2: Near Opheusden, Holland, October 1944. Lt. Hettrick's 327th Glider Infantry company holds a dike position against a German artillery bombardment and full-scale assault. Board rules: The East-West road on boards 6 (6E1-6E9) and 9 (9E1-9E9) are dike roads. Foxholes may be placed on the dike.
The German player wins by having more units on or South of row E on board 9 than the American player at the end of game turn 8.
Set up in Dike Road hexes on board 9.
The American player may exchange 1 Paratrooper DECOY for 1 additional Paratrooper Squad. If this exchange is made, American Artillery is only available on turns 5-7.
Set up on or North of row E on board 6.
